case1_source1.title: AIATSIS Map
case1_source1.caption: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Australia is made up of many different and distinct groups, each with their own culture, language and laws.
case1_source1.prompt: What does this suggest about the diversity of First Nations peoples?

case1_source2.title: Acknowledging Country
case1_source2.caption: Incorporating protocols into official meetings recognises Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples as Traditional Owners and shows respect.
case1_source2.prompt: Why is acknowledging Country more than just a formal routine?

case1_source3.title: Birds, Stories and Totems
case1_source3.caption: First Nations stories reflect a powerful worldview towards Country, valuing relationships between all living things.
case1_source3.prompt: How do stories and totems help pass on culture?

case2_source1.title: Makasar Traders
case2_source1.caption: From 1700, fishermen from Makassar (Indonesia) traded and worked with local Aboriginal people in northern Australia.
case2_source1.prompt: What does this suggest about international connections?

case2_source2.title: Trade and Transformation
case2_source2.caption: Fishermen harvested trepang (sea cucumber), processed it on shore, and traded it to China using monsoon winds.
case2_source2.prompt: What details show this trade was organised and regular?
